stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 drug treatment options. The probability of favorable patient genotype is uncertain, estimated between 34% and 47%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: Drug A: $299K in good patient genotype, $92K in bad patient genotype. Drug B: $133K in good patient genotype, $11K in bad patient genotype. Drug C: $268K in good patient genotype, $33K in bad patient genotype. combination therapy: $285K in good patient genotype, $170K in bad patient genotype. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.41: best option is combination therapy with EV=$217.2K. combination therapy is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [209.1, 224.1].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
combination therapy is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
